description: Argentina is identified as the likely exposure source area for the Andes virus cluster, with investigation focused on travel and environmental exposure around Ushuaia and Neuquen before the MV Hondius voyage.
verificationNotes: May 12 frontend update: Argentina is shown as the probable source geography rather than a newly reported active clinical case country.

description: The Netherlands is listed with the largest reported national cluster in the current frontend update, including three cases and two fatalities linked to the MV Hondius Andes virus event.
verificationNotes: May 12 frontend update based on the supplied country distribution. The Netherlands is also central to repatriation and quarantine follow-up.

description: Germany is included in the May 12 frontend update because one fatality is reported among German citizens linked to the MV Hondius Andes virus cluster.
verificationNotes: Death count is shown separately from active case counts to keep the public map aligned with the supplied May 12 country list.

description: The United Kingdom is listed with three reported cases, including the ship doctor and a guide associated with the MV Hondius voyage. Some UK-linked patients are being treated in South Africa.
verificationNotes: May 12 frontend update based on the supplied case distribution and UK-linked monitoring context.

description: South Africa is shown with one case: a critically ill MV Hondius-linked patient was evacuated via St. Helena and treated in intensive care in Johannesburg.
verificationNotes: May 20 frontend correction: South Africa is counted as 1 case and 0 deaths because the critical patient is being treated there; this does not indicate separate community transmission in South Africa.

description: Switzerland is listed with one positive case in Zurich associated with the MV Hondius Andes virus cluster and post-travel follow-up.
verificationNotes: May 12 frontend update based on the supplied country distribution.

description: Spain is shown with one new confirmed positive case after a passenger under quarantine in Madrid tested positive by PCR on May 12.
verificationNotes: The Tenerife evacuation is complete and the Madrid positive case is included in the May 12 frontend update.

description: France is shown with one new confirmed positive traveler associated with the recent cruise, as reported in the May 12 frontend update.
verificationNotes: May 12 frontend update: French health officials confirmed another traveler associated with the cruise tested positive.

description: The United States is shown with no active positive cases. Repatriated U.S. passengers remain under monitoring in Nebraska, but the previously uncertain passenger results are not counted as confirmed cases.
verificationNotes: May 20 frontend correction: CDC/WHO-aligned interpretation sets the United States to 0 cases and 0 deaths while quarantine monitoring continues.

description: Canada is listed with one confirmed MV Hondius-linked hantavirus case after a Canadian passenger tested positive following evacuation and public health follow-up.
verificationNotes: May 20 frontend update: CDC reported an additional recently identified case in Canada among the MV Hondius-linked cluster.
